Farn Por,rrrclr Pnacrrcns CovrvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
October 25,2000
Thomas J. Riggs
Lozano Smith, Attomeys atLaw
285 West Bullard Avenue, Suite 101
Fresno, Califomia 937 04-17 06
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-223
Dear Mr. Riggs:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of
interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").r
QUESTION
Should members of the State Center Community College District's Personnel
Commission be designated in the State Center Community College District's conflict of
interest code?
CONGLUSION
Yes, the commission members must be incorporated into the State Center
Community College District's conflict of interest code.
FAGTS
The State Center Community College District ("district") is a merit system district
operating pursuant to the provisions of Education Code sections 88060 et seq. It has a
personnel commission ("commission") consisting of three members. The Governing
Board of the district appoints one member, one member is nominated by the classified
(non-academic) employees of the district, and these two members appoint the third
member.
' Govemment Code sections 8 1000 - 9 1015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-00-222
Page No. 2
The commission's Education Code mandated duties include:
appointing a personnel director
adopting a budget for its office 
'
expending funds for the training and development of its staff
classiffing all employees and positions in the classified service
recommending to the Governing Board salary schedules for the classified
service
The commission also prescribes and interprets rules "to ensure the effrciency of
the service and the selection of the service and the selection and retention of employees
upon a basis of merit and fitness" (Ed. Code section 88080). The rules provide for the
piocedures to be followed by the Governing Board regarding applications examinations,
.ligibitity, appointments, promotions, demotions, transfers, dismissals, resignations, lay-
offs, and similar employment related issues for the classified service.
In addition, the commission is required to determine minimum educational and
work experience requirements for classified positions. It adopts disciplinary rules for the
classified service; and hears appeals from employees who have been suspended, demoted,
or dismissed. The commission may authorize a hearing officer to conduct any hearing or
investigation which the commission itself is authorized to conduct.
The district is a multi-county agency. As such, the Fair Political Practices
Commission is its code reviewing body.
ANALYSIS
The purpose for the disclosure and disqualification provisions of the Act is to
ensure that public officials, whether elected or appointed, perform their duties in an
impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests. (Section
81001(b).) Regulation 18701 establishes that "public official" includes a"member,
officer, employee, or consultant of a state or local govemment agency"Jemphasis added).
The regulation further states that:
"Member" shall include, but not be limited to, salaried or
unsalaried members of boards or commissions with
I
I
I
t


File No. A-00-222
Page No. 3
decisionmaking authority. A board or commission possesses
decisionmaking authority whenever:
(A) It may make a final govemmental decision
(B) It may compel a govenrmental decision; or it
may prevent a govemmental decision either by reason of
an exclusive power to initiate the decision o, it;;;;"
of a veto which may not be overridden; or
(C) It makes substantive recommendations which

agency
(Regutation 1 870 I (a)(1).)

material effect on any financial interest . . . .,, (sec tion g73t02(a).) Regulation rg7o2.r
assists in defining what it is to make u gorr*"rrtal decision. A public official makes a
govemmental decision when he or she:-
. 
Votes on a matter;
' 
Appoints a person;
a

Enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her

Because the commissio.n h-as, among other,authorities, the power to appoint to positions,
hire consultants, adopt budgets, tp"iJn nas-for training, "It., the agency into contracts,

interests. For examote, as slta:tea above,the commissio, rr", ,rr" authority to expend
funds for haining' certainly, it *ouraie in the public;s inirest to know if a commission


File No. A-00-222
Page No. 4
member has a financial interest in a training company. Therefore, the district must
amend its conflict of interest code to include the commission members.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, you may contact me toll-
free at 8661275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
